fundamentals.name: DoorDash, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: DoorDash, Inc., together with its subsidiaries, operates a commerce platform that connects merchants, consumers, and dashers in the United States and internationally. The company operates DoorDash Marketplace, Wolt Marketplace, and Deliveroo Marketplace, which provide various services, such as customer acquisition, demand generation, order fulfillment, merchandising, payment processing, and customer support. It also offers consumer membership programs, DashPass, Wolt+, and Deliveroo Plus; advertising as a value-added service through its marketplaces; and white-label delivery fulfillment services, as well as services that help merchants establish online ordering, build branded mobile apps, manage reservations and in-store dining, manage consumer relationships, enable tableside order and pay, and improve customer support. The company was formerly known as Palo Alto Delivery Inc. and changed its name to DoorDash, Inc. in 2015. DoorDash, Inc. was founded in 2013 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and strong free cash flow are offset by a high valuation and recent negative earnings growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates with EPS of 0.42 vs 0.36; next earnings date is 2026-05-06.
positives: Revenue growth 33.1% YoY — strong top-line expansion.
Forward P/E 20.46x — significantly lower than trailing P/E, indicating expected earnings growth.
Free cash flow $2.30B TTM — healthy cash generation.
Gross margin 51.8% — robust profitability on services.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a high mean target of $245.99.
negatives: Trailing P/E 75.14x — valuation appears stretched based on past performance.
Earnings growth -6.1% YoY and quarterly earnings growth -4.7% — profitability is declining.
Analyst growth estimate for current Q is -13.0% — near-term earnings contraction expected.
Debt/Equity 32.19% — leverage is notable, though manageable given cash flow.
Beta 1.87 — stock is more volatile than the broader market.

news.paragraph: DoorDash is gaining traction with expansion talks in the Middle East and a new deal with Dollar Tree, which is expected to boost delivery volume. This comes as Jim Cramer has publicly endorsed the stock as a buy, adding to positive sentiment. However, an insider sell from Keith Yandell was also reported, introducing a note of caution. The company also launched a global campaign for the FIFA World Cup 2026, aiming to leverage major sporting events for growth.
